_meta.description: Contact and NPC voice personality profiles for coh_npc_voices (Sidekick)
_meta.source: City of Heroes Homecoming — common contacts across hero and villain sides
notes: Voice IDs here are descriptive archetypes, not provider-specific IDs.
Each TTS engine plugin maps archetype -> its own voice ID in its config.
Clockwork King and generic Clockwork faction already handled by Sidekick's built-in vocoder — not duplicated here.
Entries ordered by estimated player contact frequency.

contacts.name: Ms. Liberty
contacts.personality: Confident, authoritative, military bearing. Brisk delivery. Believes in the letter of law and civic duty. No-nonsense but respectful.
contacts.notes: Daughter of Statesman. Voice should convey institutional authority, not warmth.

contacts.name: Statesman
contacts.personality: The hero ideal. Measured, deliberate, deeply moral. Never brash. Carries the weight of decades of leadership.
contacts.notes: Classic American hero voice. Think Walter Cronkite energy, not action movie energy.

contacts.name: Sister Psyche
contacts.personality: Warm, perceptive, slightly formal. Speaks with the careful precision of someone who reads emotions. Compassionate but measured.
contacts.notes: Should feel like a therapist who knows exactly what you're feeling. Not bubbly — wise.

contacts.name: Synapse
contacts.personality: Enthusiastic, rapid-fire, slightly scattered. His thoughts come faster than his mouth. Jokes don't always land but he doesn't notice.
contacts.notes: Speed powerset personality bleeds into his speech patterns. Should feel slightly breathless.

contacts.name: Numina
contacts.personality: Otherworldly, serene, slightly sad. She exists partially outside the physical world. Speaks in longer sentences with thoughtful pauses.
contacts.notes: Ghost/spirit character. Breathy, ethereal tone. The only living member of the Phalanx who's technically dead.

contacts.name: Back Alley Brawler
contacts.personality: Blunt, practical, working-class. Zero patience for pretense. Direct to the point of seeming rude. Genuinely cares but won't show it.
contacts.notes: Brooklyn/South Side energy. Contrast with Statesman's gravitas — same team, completely different register.

contacts.name: Positron
contacts.personality: Scientific, analytical, occasionally pedantic. Explains things more thoroughly than necessary. Genuinely excited by problems.
contacts.notes: Speaks through a suit — slight metallic resonance appropriate. Nuclear physicist energy.

contacts.name: Ghost Widow
contacts.personality: Cold, deliberate, formal. Zero warmth — not cruelty, just complete emotional absence. Every word precisely chosen.
contacts.notes: Contrast with Numina — also dead, completely different register. Numina is sad; Ghost Widow is empty.

contacts.name: Lord Recluse
contacts.personality: Grand, theatrical, savors every word. Believes in Social Darwinism with religious conviction. Villain monologue energy always present.
contacts.notes: The main villain of CoV. Should feel like every sentence is a speech. Never hurried.

contacts.name: Black Scorpion
contacts.personality: Aggressive, loud, prone to threats. Not subtle. The muscle, not the brain. Short sentences. Impatient.
contacts.notes: Military mercenary type who joined Arachnos for the firepower. Contrast with Ghost Widow's precision.

contacts.name: Scirocco
contacts.personality: Melancholic, poetic, regretful. A former hero who made the wrong choice long ago and knows it. Speaks with the cadence of someone who has given up on being saved.
contacts.notes: Italian accent appropriate. The tragic figure of CoV. Most sympathetic Arachnos patron.

contacts.name: Mako
contacts.personality: Predatory, barely contained, excited by violence. Speaks in short bursts. Can go from conversational to threatening instantly.
contacts.notes: Shark person. The most 'monster' of the Arachnos patrons. Restraint in delivery makes the implied threat worse.

contacts.name: Dr. Aeon
contacts.personality: Manic, excited, talks too fast when something interests him. Mad scientist register — brilliant but unstable. Laughs at inappropriate moments.
contacts.notes: Zone controller for Cap au Diable. Should feel like he's three ideas ahead of where he's talking.

contacts.name: Arbiter Sands
contacts.personality: Procedural, dry, everything is policy. Not cruel — just utterly impersonal. Treats heroes like paperwork.
contacts.notes: The Arachnos middle-management experience. Flat affect. References regulations often.

contacts.name: Penny Preston
contacts.personality: Enthusiastic, optimistic, idealistic. Aspiring journalist who hasn't had those qualities crushed yet. Excited to be near heroes.
contacts.notes: Contrast with the world-weary tone of most contacts. Her optimism should feel genuine, not naive.

contacts.name: Coyote
contacts.personality: Casual, irreverent, enjoys the chaos. Speaks like someone who knows the joke everyone else is missing. Never fully serious.
contacts.notes: Trickster archetype. Wry humor. Never answers directly when a sideways answer will do.

contacts.name: Jim Temblor
contacts.personality: Experienced, no-nonsense, seen everything. Short sentences. Does not over-explain. Respects competence, ignores posturing.
contacts.notes: Old-school hero energy. Has been doing this for decades and is tired of rookies.

contacts.name: Billie Heck
contacts.personality: Pragmatic, street-level, deals with reality not ideals. Not unkind but not soft. Knows the neighborhood.
contacts.notes: Working-class hero contact. Less formal than Atlas Park heroes.

contacts.name: Jose Brogan
contacts.personality: Practical, salt-of-the-earth. Doesn't trust things he can't see. Talks about problems like a contractor would — what needs fixing, how, in what order.
contacts.notes: Steel Canyon contact. Blue-collar Paragon City energy.

contacts.name: Calvin Scott
contacts.personality: Community-minded, earnest, driven by genuine care for residents. Political but not cynical. Speaks in terms of people, not power.
contacts.notes: Faultline arc contact. Grief-motivated, community focus.

contacts.name: Sunstorm
contacts.personality: Formal, alien cadence, chooses words with visible care. Not cold — deeply compassionate but expresses it through duty. Language is slightly structured.
contacts.notes: Kheldian Peacebringer contact. Should feel non-human without being robotic.

contacts.name: Requiem
contacts.personality: Ancient, cold, patient. Has been planning for centuries. Every sentence suggests he knows more than he's saying. No urgency — he's waited this long.
contacts.notes: Council arc villain. Should feel genuinely ancient, not just old.

contacts.name: Renault
contacts.personality: Charming, smooth, always has an angle. Never raises his voice. Makes manipulation look like a favor.
contacts.notes: The fixer type. Everything is a negotiation.

contacts.name: Technician Naylor
contacts.personality: Technical, efficient, slight condescension toward non-technical people. Not unkind — just used to being the smartest person in the room.
contacts.notes: Tech contact. Precision in word choice mirrors precision in engineering.

faction_defaults._note: When an NPC name is unrecognized, use these faction-level defaults based on what appears before the ':' in the log line.

Rikti.notes: Rikti speak in clipped noun phrases: 'Heroes: identified. Threat: minimal. Elimination: proceeding.' TTS should reflect this syntax.

Nemesis.notes: Nemesis soldiers speak in 19th century military formalism. Automaton variants may warrant vocoder.
